perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Agreement Term The term "Agreement Term" shall mean the period of time commencing on the Effective Date and, unless this Agreement is terminated sooner as provided in Article 17, expiring on the date when all work has been completed or terminated under all R&D Plans.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Agreement Term The term "Agreement Term" shall mean the period of time commencing on the Effective Date and expiring after 10 years.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'unless this Agreement is terminated sooner as provided in Article 17, expiring on the date when all work has been completed or terminated under all R&D Plans.' we create an in-text contradiction because Article 17 allows for termination under specific conditions, but the changed text stipulates that the agreement term expires after 10 years regardless of early termination scenarios, leading to uncertainty in enforcement of termination clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17.2 Termination 17.2.1 Termination for Breach A Party ("Non-Breaching Party") shall have the right to terminate this Agreement on a Work Stream-by-Work Stream basis, or, for Roche, on an Approved Marker or Investigational Marker basis in the case of the CDx Development Program, in the event the other Party ("Breaching Party") is in material breach of any of its material obligations under the applicable Work Stream (or obligations pertaining to an Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program). Failure of FMI to comply materially with Performance Specifications or Quality Standards shall be considered a material breach by FMI. For avoidance of doubt, a Non-Breaching Party shall only be permitted to terminate the Work Stream (or Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program) to which a material breach of a material obligation relates. The Non-Breaching Party shall provide written notice to the Breaching Party, which notice shall identify the breach. Except in the event of a breach that, by its nature, is not amenable to cure, in which case termination may be made effective immediately, the Breaching Party shall have a period of […***…] after such written notice is provided ("Peremptory Notice Period") to cure such breach or, absent withdrawal of the Non-Breaching Party's request for termination, the relevant Work Stream (or Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program) shall terminate; provided that, if the Breaching Party has a bona fide dispute as to whether such breach: (i) occurred, (ii) pertains to a material obligation, or (iii) has been cured, the Breaching Party will so notify the Non-Breaching Party, the relevant Work Stream (or Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program) shall not terminate and the expiration of the Peremptory Notice Period shall be tolled until such dispute is resolved pursuant to Section 19.2. If such dispute is resolved by finding that the Non-Breaching Party is entitled to terminate the relevant Work Stream (or Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program), the Breaching Party may have the remainder of the Peremptory Notice Period to cure such breach. If such breach is not cured within the Peremptory Notice Period, then absent withdrawal of the Non-Breaching Party's request for termination, the relevant Work Stream (or Approved Marker or Investigational Marker program) shall terminate in accordance with the notice from the Non-Breaching Party as of the expiration of the Peremptory Notice Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination of the agreement prior to the completion of all R&D plans, as detailed in Article 17. The changed text removes this possibility, stating the agreement expires after 10 years regardless of Article 17. This directly contradicts section 17.2, which outlines conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability of the termination clauses.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Obligation Not to Disclose Confidential Information 16.1 Non-Use and Non-Disclosure During the Agreement Term and for […***…] thereafter, a Receiving Party shall (i) treat Confidential Information provided by Disclosing Party as it would treat its own information of a similar nature, (ii) take all reasonable precautions not to disclose such Confidential Information ***Confidential Treatment Requested*** - 51 - to Third Parties, without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and (iii) not use such Confidential Information other than for fulfilling its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Obligation Not to Disclose Confidential Information 16.1 Non-Use and Non-Disclosure During the Agreement Term, a Receiving Party shall (i) treat Confidential Information provided by Disclosing Party as it would treat its own information of a similar nature, (ii) take all reasonable precautions not to disclose such Confidential Information ***Confidential Treatment Requested*** - 51 - to Third Parties, without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and (iii) not use such Confidential Information other than for fulfilling its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and for […***…] thereafter', we create an in-text contradiction because section 17.5 explicitly states the survival of Article 16 after the termination of the agreement. This is because the changed text limits the non-disclosure obligation to only the 'Agreement Term,' conflicting with the post-termination survival clause in Section 17.5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17.5 Survival In addition to any provisions that expressly survive in accordance with Article 17.3, Article 1 (Definitions, to the extent necessary to interpret the Agreement), Section 3.5.2 (Sample Handling and Disposal, to the extent applicable), Section 10 (Taxes), Section 12.1 (Ownership of Inventions), Section 12.9 (Common Interest Disclosures), Section 13.8.11 (Survival of Privacy and Security Obligations), Article 14 (Indemnification), Article 16 (Obligation Not to Disclose Confidential Information), Section 17.3 (Consequences of Termination), Section 17.5 (Surival), Section 19.1 (Governing Law), and Section 19.3 (Arbitration) shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any provision of this Agreement that is intended by its very nature to survive expiration or termination of this Agreement shall also survive.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'and for […***…] thereafter' from section 16.1, limiting the non-disclosure obligation to only the 'Agreement Term'. Section 17.5 explicitly states that Article 16 (Obligation Not to Disclose Confidential Information) survives any expiration or termination of the agreement. Therefore, the changed text in 16.1 directly contradicts the survival clause in 17.5.

perturbation.original_text: 8.7 Disclosure of Payments FMI acknowledges that Roche may be obligated to disclose this financial arrangement, including all fees, payments and transfers of value, as may be advisable or required under Applicable Law, including the US Sunshine Act.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'FMI acknowledges that Roche may be obligated to disclose this financial arrangement, including all fees, payments and transfers of value, as may be advisable or required under Applicable Law, including the US Sunshine Act.', the contract no longer explicitly acknowledges Roche's obligations under disclosure laws like the US Sunshine Act, potentially leading to ambiguity about reporting requirements and compliance, which may conflict with Roche's actual legal obligations under the US Sunshine Act. By removing this statement, and other sections of the contract rely on the assumption that Roche will comply with all relevant laws. Without this statement, the assumption isn't reffered and creates a contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2 Activities Each Party will perform all activities under this Agreement (i) in a professional manner, (ii) in conformance with the level or care and skill ordinarily exercised by other professional institutions in similar circumstances, and (iii) in compliance with Applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the clause acknowledging Roche's obligation to disclose financial arrangements under applicable law, including the US Sunshine Act, directly contradicts clause 13.2, which mandates that each party performs all activities in compliance with applicable law. The removal weakens the explicit acknowledgment of Roche's legal disclosure obligations, making it unclear whether Roche is still required to adhere to them, thus creating a contradiction with the requirement of compliance in 13.2. The original text made clear Roche's obligations but the Changed text created a strong contradiction to the contradicted text.
